comment: Building blocks for preset trips: one block = one coherent day. zone: sw = doable on a Reykjavík-based trip, ring = needs the full circle. order = position along the trip flow (sw: out-and-back; ring: clockwise). level: 1 greatest hits · 2 explorer · 3 off the beaten path. months like attractions (Jan-first 0–3). stops = curated attraction ids in visit order (validated).

blocks.title: Reykjavík in a day
blocks.blurb: The walkable capital: the rocket church, the glass concert hall, harbour food and the best indoor primer on Icelandic nature.

blocks.title: Capital soak & shore
blocks.blurb: A slow day the local way: city pool culture, the Grótta shore walk, then an oceanfront lagoon at dusk — aurora season gold.

blocks.title: Reykjanes & the Blue Lagoon
blocks.blurb: The UNESCO geopark next to the airport: rift lake, boiling mud, the continents' bridge — finished in the milky blue water.

blocks.title: Raw Reykjanes
blocks.blurb: Fresh lava underfoot: hike the Fagradalsfjall eruption fields, then the surf-carved coast — the peninsula without the spa crowds.

blocks.title: The Golden Circle
blocks.blurb: The classic loop: the rift-valley parliament plain, Strokkur erupting on schedule, the golden falls, and a crater lake to finish.

blocks.title: Golden Circle, quiet edition
blocks.blurb: Same country, fewer buses: the bluest brook in Iceland, tomato-greenhouse lunch, and a 19th-century pool that steams in any weather.

blocks.title: Þjórsárdalur valley
blocks.blurb: The valley most drive past: twin falls in basalt, the hidden elf-garden of Gjáin, and Háifoss thundering into its huge canyon.

blocks.title: Kjölur highlands
blocks.blurb: 4x4 up the Kjölur route between two ice caps: steaming Hveradalir ridges at Kerlingarfjöll, then an outlaw's hot pool at Hveravellir.

blocks.title: Landmannalaugar
blocks.blurb: Rhyolite mountains in every color and a natural hot river to soak in after — the highlands' crown jewel, F-roads and all.

blocks.title: South coast waterfalls
blocks.blurb: The greatest-hits corridor: walk behind Seljalandsfoss, find its hidden neighbour, stand under Skógafoss, touch a glacier tongue.

blocks.title: South coast, hidden layer
blocks.blurb: Behind the famous stops: a fall you'll have to yourself, a 1923 mountain pool, the DC-3 wreck walk, and Iceland's best folk museum.

blocks.title: Þórsmörk
blocks.blurb: Thor's valley by highland bus or super-jeep: birch forests walled by three glaciers, slot-canyon walks, big-view ridges.

blocks.title: Dyrhólaey & the black beach
blocks.blurb: The sea-arch headland (puffins in summer), the basalt columns and thundering surf of Reynisfjara, and Iceland's southernmost village.

blocks.title: Westman Islands
blocks.blurb: Ferry to the island half-buried in 1973: climb the still-warm volcano, meet Europe's biggest puffin colony, feel a town on the edge.

blocks.title: Moss canyons to Klaustur
blocks.blurb: The serpentine moss-walled gorge of Fjaðrárgljúfur, then across the lava flood plains to the little church-floor town.

blocks.title: Glacier lagoon day
blocks.blurb: Vatnajökull's front row: Skaftafell's trails, icebergs drifting out of Jökulsárlón, and ice diamonds glittering on black sand.

blocks.title: Skaftafell on foot
blocks.blurb: Earn the views: the basalt-organ falls of Svartifoss, a crevassed glacier snout, the quieter lagoon, and the canyon nobody tells you about.

blocks.title: Stokksnes & Höfn
blocks.blurb: The photographers' mountain rising from a black tidal flat, then langoustine in the harbour town under Vatnajökull.

blocks.title: East Fjords villages
blocks.blurb: The slow coast: granite eggs on a harbour road, one woman's legendary stone collection, and the rainbow street of Seyðisfjörður.

blocks.title: Stuðlagil & Hengifoss
blocks.blurb: Basalt colonnades over a teal river, the red-striped giant Hengifoss, and floating pools on a lake to finish.

blocks.title: Borgarfjörður eystri puffins
blocks.blurb: Boardwalks through ten thousand puffin burrows at arm's length — the closest, calmest puffin encounter in Iceland.

blocks.title: Dettifoss & Ásbyrgi
blocks.blurb: Europe's most powerful waterfall throwing spray a kilometer, then the echoing horseshoe canyon said to be Sleipnir's hoofprint.

blocks.title: Húsavík whales
blocks.blurb: Europe's whale-watching capital: humpbacks on Skjálfandi bay, then hot seawater infinity pools watching for spouts from the cliff.

blocks.title: Mývatn's boiling earth
blocks.blurb: A volcanic wonderland in one loop: Mars-orange mud cauldrons, a rift cave, the Dark Fortresses, and milky-blue baths at dusk.

blocks.title: Askja expedition
blocks.blurb: A serious F-road day deep into the interior: the vast caldera where Apollo astronauts trained, and a milky crater you can swim in.

blocks.title: Goðafoss to Akureyri
blocks.blurb: The waterfall of the gods on the way into the capital of the north — botanical garden, food street, and forest pools across the fjord.

blocks.title: Tröllaskagi & the herring coast
blocks.blurb: Single-lane tunnels to the old herring capital, the fjord-edge infinity pool at Hofsós, and the grass-roofed farm at Glaumbær.

blocks.title: Vatnsnes & the troll rock
blocks.blurb: The drinking-dragon sea stack with seals hauled out nearby, and a stacked little waterfall gorge nobody stops at.

blocks.title: Dynjandi & Ísafjörður
blocks.blurb: The Westfjords' bridal-veil giant and the sandspit town under 700-meter walls — Iceland with the crowds deleted.

blocks.title: Látrabjarg & the red sands
blocks.blurb: Europe's westernmost cliffs stacked with millions of seabirds, and ten kilometers of impossibly un-Icelandic red-gold beach.

blocks.title: Glymur hike
blocks.blurb: Iceland's second-highest waterfall the honest way: a cave, a log river-crossing, and vertiginous canyon-edge paths. 3–4 hours.

blocks.title: Borgarfjörður falls & lava
blocks.blurb: Europe's mightiest hot spring, a kilometer of water seeping straight out of a lava field, a climbable crater — then Krauma's pools.

blocks.title: Into the glacier
blocks.blurb: Snorri's saga country, then a monster truck onto Langjökull and 30 meters down into blue ice — the year-round glacier day.

blocks.title: Snæfellsnes classics
blocks.blurb: Iceland in miniature: the arrowhead mountain, pearl-black beaches, cliff arches and the lone black church — all under Jules Verne's volcano.

blocks.title: Snæfellsnes, deeper
blocks.blurb: The quieter layer: seals at Ytri-Tunga, a troll's cleft in the mountainside, a lava tube by lantern, and mussels in Stykkishólmur.
